title: Decide the final research claim
rationale: The current official final artifact does not support the intended AI energy-optimization headline.
exact_fix: Either improve and rerun the final model/matrix until the claim is true, or rewrite the capstone narrative, README, demo copy, and report so they match the actual evidence.
done_when: The written claim in the repo and report exactly matches the strongest included evidence.

title: Package one reproducible final evidence bundle
rationale: Summary tables are not enough if the referenced run folders and checkpoint are absent from the repo.
exact_fix: Add one canonical final bundle containing the official summary tables, the exact checkpoint used, and either the referenced run folders or a manifest that maps every summary row to included artifacts.
done_when: A reviewer can trace final report tables to bundled run evidence without missing files.

title: Unify the setup and run instructions
rationale: Current docs disagree with the repo and create immediate reviewer friction.
exact_fix: Rewrite README, COMMANDS, and frontend docs around one current environment, one training path, one evaluation path, one demo path, and one packaging path.
done_when: A fresh reviewer can follow the docs without encountering missing files, wrong paths, or contradictory commands.

title: Define a complete reproducible environment
rationale: The repo uses packages that are not fully declared and the current environment could not run basic verification.
exact_fix: Add a documented dependency strategy for core runtime, RL/ML extras, tests, and optional plotting/frontend prerequisites, then verify it from a clean environment.
done_when: The documented install path supports API import, test execution, and supported evaluation scripts.

title: Make basic verification actually pass
evidence: `npm run build` in frontend -> node.exe unavailable
rationale: Submission readiness requires demonstrable operability, not just code presence.
exact_fix: Fix the environment, then run and document at least one Python test pass, one API import/startup check, and one frontend build check.
done_when: The repo contains a verified smoke-test section with commands and successful outcomes.

title: Resolve the Impact Predictor scope mismatch
rationale: README-level claims about a completed accepted subsystem are risky if the artifacts are missing.
exact_fix: Either bundle the runtime model and acceptance artifacts, or explicitly downgrade the subsystem to optional/experimental and remove completion framing.
done_when: The documented state of Impact Predictor matches the actual included repo evidence.

title: Explain the real controller architecture honestly
rationale: The RL controller is not acting alone; safety gates and optional learned risk gating materially affect behavior.
exact_fix: Add a short architecture section in the docs/report describing the actual runtime control stack as RL plus hard safety constraints plus optional learned risk gating.
done_when: A reviewer can understand what produces the final action at runtime without inferring it from code.

title: Reconcile locked validation evidence with the final thesis result
rationale: The repo contains both PASS traffic-verification artifacts and a failed final hypothesis, which is confusing without explanation.
exact_fix: Add a short explanatory note distinguishing scenario safety validation from the thesis headline baseline-vs-AI evaluation.
done_when: The repo explicitly explains why both kinds of evidence exist and what each one proves.

title: Choose one canonical config family
rationale: Too many versioned root configs make the final experimental setup unclear.
exact_fix: Keep one canonical final config set and move the rest into an archive folder or label them as historical.
done_when: A reviewer can identify the exact final config files without guessing.

title: Mark legacy entrypoints and internal tools explicitly
rationale: Too many active-looking entrypoints create workflow ambiguity.
exact_fix: Label legacy scripts as archived/internal, keep only the official path prominent, and state the role of the Streamlit dashboard clearly.
done_when: The repo has one obvious official path through training, evaluation, and demo.

title: Fix demo fallback integrity
rationale: Silent synthetic fallback can mislead reviewers during a demo.
exact_fix: Make demo mode explicit in the UI, prevent silent substitution for backend-backed results, and clearly separate synthetic demo data from real experiment artifacts.
done_when: A reviewer can always tell whether the UI is showing real backend data or demo-generated data.

title: Fix policy alias consistency across the frontend demo path
rationale: Incorrect policy alias mapping can mislabel results in the exact place a reviewer sees them.
exact_fix: Use one canonical alias map everywhere across backend, frontend, demo, and reporting utilities.
done_when: The same historical policy labels resolve to the same canonical policies everywhere in the repo.

title: Clean reviewer-facing artifacts of machine-specific paths
rationale: Absolute local paths and workstation-specific provenance make the package look unpolished.
exact_fix: Regenerate or edit final documentation/artifacts to use relative or generic paths only.
done_when: No reviewer-facing docs or final artifacts expose local workstation paths.

title: Add a concise final architecture and artifact glossary
rationale: The codebase has real structure, but it is hard to understand quickly from the current docs.
exact_fix: Add one compact section describing module roles and another describing what each artifact folder contains.
done_when: A new reviewer can understand the repo layout in under five minutes.

title: Add a limitations and responsible-design section
rationale: The project already encodes QoS safeguards, but the final submission should show that energy optimization is constrained by service quality and safety.
exact_fix: Add a concise limitations/responsible-design section to the README and final report explaining QoS constraints, safety gates, and the possibility of negative or mixed AI results.
done_when: The repo explicitly frames the project as constrained optimization with clear limitations, not unconstrained energy minimization.
